sections.paragraph:
(“peaceable,” from שְׁלֹום with the addition of the syllable הֹ i.q. ֹו, ֹון compare 1Ch 22:9) pr.n. Solomon, the tenth son of David ( 1Ch 3:5 compare 2Sa 3:5), born of Bathsheba; his father’s successor, and the third king of the Israelites (1005-975, b.c.); very celebrated for his riches, splendour, and wisdom; see -1 Ki. 211 ; 1Ch 23:1-32 -2Ch 19:1-11; Pro 1:1 Son 1:1. LXX. Σαλωμών; called by Josephus, and in N. T., Σολομών.

[שַׁלְמַי Shalmai, pr.n. m. Ezr 2:46.]
